introSection: Kenny's Forest Flyer is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Queensland, Australia.

sections.title: History
sections.content: When it first opened in 2002, the ride was called Rugrats Runaway Reptar. In 2011, with the debranding of Nickelodeon Central into Kid's World, the ride was renamed Sky Rocket. With the 2012 addition of the DreamWorks Experience, the ride was renamed Escape from Madagascar. After the announcement of the Kenny and Belinda's Dreamland area in November 2022, the ride was temporarily named "Escape Coaster" before closing for refurbishment in April 2023, and reopened in August 2023 under its new name - Kenny's Forest Flyer.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single train with 10 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 20 riders.

fullText:
Kenny's Forest Flyer is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Queensland, Australia.

When it first opened in 2002, the ride was called Rugrats Runaway Reptar. In 2011, with the debranding of Nickelodeon Central into Kid's World, the ride was renamed Sky Rocket. With the 2012 addition of the DreamWorks Experience, the ride was renamed Escape from Madagascar. After the announcement of the Kenny and Belinda's Dreamland area in November 2022, the ride was temporarily named "Escape Coaster" before closing for refurbishment in April 2023, and reopened in August 2023 under its new name - Kenny's Forest Flyer.



Single train with 10 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 20 riders.
